title: Long-Baseline VLF Observations of Solar Flares from Antarctica
summary:
arXiv:2607.13913v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We present long-baseline Very Low Frequency (VLF) observations of solar flare-induced ionospheric disturbances obtained at the Bulgarian Polar Astronomical Observatory (St. Kliment Ohridski Base) on Livingston island, Antarctica. Using continuous VLF transmissions at 21.4 kHz (NPM, Hawaii) and 24.0 kHz (NAA, Maine), propagating over trans-hemispheric paths exceeding 11000 km, we analyze observations of solar flares during the period 24 January--8 February 2025.
